authoritative_sources.title: NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST CSF 2.0 is the official cross-industry cybersecurity risk-management framework for organizations and governments, so it is authoritative for outcome language, framework profile structure, and cybersecurity evidence context.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: control baseline intake and evidence outcome framing
used_for: mapping documentation and artifacts to Govern, Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, and Recover outcomes
ensuring cross-industry cybersecurity is treated as concrete control evidence rather than generic documentation
source-backed caveats that framework mappings are readiness aids, not formal certification

authoritative_sources.title: CISA Cross-Sector Cybersecurity Performance Goals
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA is the U.S. federal cyber defense agency, and the Cross-Sector CPGs provide official prioritized cybersecurity practices and outcomes for critical infrastructure and cross-sector organizations.
used_for: prioritizing evidence gaps for high-impact cyber practices
identifying documentation gaps that materially affect operational resilience
grounding corrective-action escalation without expanding into implementation authority

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-37 Rev. 2: Risk Management Framework for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST RMF is the official prepare, categorize, select, implement, assess, authorize, and monitor workflow for security and privacy risk management, directly constraining evidence lifecycle and authorization boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: documentation lifecycle, POA&M, authorization-boundary, and continuous-monitoring handoff
used_for: structuring evidence lifecycle stages and monitoring records
separating evidence package readiness from authorization to operate or risk acceptance
defining hold and corrective-action escalation when assessment or monitoring records are missing

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-53A Rev. 5: Assessing Security and Privacy Controls in Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53A is official control assessment guidance defining assessment objectives and methods, so it is authoritative for evidence sufficiency vocabulary and review notes while this lane avoids formal testing or attestation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: control evidence inspection and sufficiency review
used_for: classifying evidence needs using examination, interview, and test vocabulary
marking evidence as supported, partial, unsupported, stale, conflicting, or blocked
preventing control review support from becoming formal control testing or assessor signoff

authoritative_sources.title: Cyber Resilience Review (CRR)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA CRR is an official resilience assessment service and method, making it authoritative for resilience-domain evidence, service-dependency records, incident and continuity artifacts, and readiness-gap handling.
used_for: identifying service, dependency, incident, continuity, and resilience evidence gaps
defining failure modes where incident or operational records are absent
routing live response execution outside the documentation lane

authoritative_sources.title: Description Criteria for Management's Description of an Entity's Cybersecurity Risk Management Program
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA ASEC developed the criteria for management descriptions of cybersecurity risk management programs, directly governing cybersecurity documentation package shape while CPA assurance remains outside this lane.
used_for: organizing cybersecurity program descriptions, policies, processes, and control evidence
mapping management-prepared documentation to review-ready package sections
preserving the boundary between documentation support and formal attestation

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5, Update 1: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 is the official security and privacy control catalog used by federal and commercial programs for control family terminology, control implementation statements, and evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: control-to-evidence mapping and artifact gap analysis
used_for: mapping controls to evidence artifacts and control owners
defining artifact expectations for access, logging, configuration, vulnerability, incident, and recovery domains
supporting repeat-defect and nonconformance categorization

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-171 Rev. 3: Protecting Controlled Unclassified Information in Nonfederal Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-171 Rev. 3 is the official CUI security requirement source for nonfederal systems and directly constrains SSP, POA&M, inherited-control, and CUI evidence package scenarios.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: CUI evidence package, SSP, and POA&M review
used_for: requiring SSP, POA&M, CUI boundary, implementation statement, and inherited-control evidence
recording government and assessor acceptance caveats
supporting industry-specific artifact scenarios under the cross-industry profile

authoritative_sources.title: CIS Critical Security Controls Version 8
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CIS Controls v8 are a recognized community-developed cybersecurity control framework with prioritized safeguards and implementation groups, making them authoritative for practical evidence inventory and remediation sequencing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: safeguard-level evidence inventory and remediation prioritization
used_for: translating safeguards into artifact requests
prioritizing evidence gaps using implementation groups and safeguard criticality
crosswalking operational evidence to NIST and tenant controls without claiming equivalence

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP Logging Cheat Sheet
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP logging guidance is a recognized vendor-neutral application security source for event logging, log attributes, protection, and verification, directly relevant when logs are used as audit evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: log evidence sufficiency and retention proof review
used_for: checking log evidence completeness, coverage, and protection caveats
identifying missing event fields, retention proof, or integrity proof
routing operational logging changes to implementation owners

authoritative_sources.title: 2017 Trust Services Criteria with Revised Points of Focus - 2022
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA Trust Services Criteria are used in SOC 2 and SOC for Cybersecurity contexts for security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy criteria, making them relevant to criteria-to-evidence package structure while attestation remains out of scope.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: trust-services criteria evidence mapping and SOC-readiness caveats
used_for: mapping SOC-style criteria to management evidence
separating package readiness from CPA opinion or report issuance
identifying criteria, points of focus, and evidence gaps for human auditor review

workflow_stages.name: scope, authority, and source intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the control set, assurance target, system boundary, tenant entity, data classification, evidence confidence threshold, source freshness, customer or assessor criteria, and authority matrix before producing documentation or evidence guidance.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: control baseline and package criteria selection
workflow_stages.description: Select public and tenant-approved control criteria, record source precedence, identify conflicts, and define package sections for policies, procedures, control implementation statements, logs, tickets, SSPs, POA&Ms, trust-services criteria, and reviewer notes.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: evidence inventory and systems-of-record retrieval
workflow_stages.description: Gather artifact IDs, owners, dates, retention status, review status, control references, incident or finding links, and source-system provenance from GRC, evidence, ticketing, SIEM, identity, cloud, vulnerability, and document-management systems.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: control inspection and sufficiency review
workflow_stages.description: Inspect evidence against selected controls and classify each item as supported, partially supported, unsupported, stale, conflicting, inherited-proof-needed, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing facts. Use assessment vocabulary without performing formal testing or attestation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: gap prioritization, hold posture, and corrective-action escalation
workflow_stages.description: Rank gaps by control criticality, evidence weakness, recurrence, operational or assurance impact, source conflict, and dependency; maintain holds when evidence is insufficient; and route corrective actions to owners with proof and approval requirements.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: package assembly, tenant adaptation, and handoff
workflow_stages.description: Produce the evidence matrix, gapped evidence list, readiness note, package index, assumptions, unresolved ambiguity, tenant adaptation constraints, and orchestrator handoff when final authority belongs to legal, compliance, auditor, assessor, architecture, incident response, penetration testing, or executive approval owners.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: assessment request, scope statement, system or service boundary, tenant entity, assurance target, confidence threshold, customer or assessor criteria, and approval matrix

control sets, framework profiles, NIST CSF outcomes, NIST SP 800-53 controls, NIST SP 800-171 requirements, CIS safeguards, CISA CPG references, OWASP logging expectations, AICPA criteria, and tenant control matrices
policies, standards, procedures, runbooks, SSPs, POA&Ms, control implementation statements, exception registers, risk registers, and remediation plans

evidence inventories, artifact indexes, screenshots, exports, SIEM logs, access reviews, change tickets, vulnerability reports, backup restore logs, training records, configuration records, and owner signoffs

finding logs, review notes, repeat-defect records, incident summaries, nonconformance records, management responses, corrective-action tickets, hold records, release-readiness notes, and human signoff records

mapped evidence matrix, gapped evidence list, quality or safety review note, control or incident summary, corrective-action escalation note, compliance-ready package recommendation, and boundary-safe escalation package
systems_of_record: GRC platform, control library, obligation register, risk register, exception register, and control-owner registry
policy, standard, procedure, runbook, SSP, POA&M, and control-matrix repository
evidence repository, workpaper store, document management system, customer assurance portal, and audit request tracker
ticketing, change-management, remediation backlog, corrective-action tracking, and issue-tracking systems

SIEM, logging platform, audit-log archive, monitoring tool, vulnerability-management system, endpoint platform, identity platform, cloud control plane, network management system, backup platform, and configuration repository
vendor assurance repository, SOC report store, PCI AoC repository, inherited-control evidence store, and shared-responsibility records
findings log, nonconformance tracker, incident summary register, hold or release tracker, management response log, and human signoff records

allowed: classify security documentation and audit-evidence requests as in-scope, blocked on missing prerequisites, out-of-scope, or adjacent-specialist handoff
select and document source-backed evidence criteria and tenant-specific source precedence assumptions
map controls, outcomes, criteria, policies, procedures, logs, tickets, SSPs, POA&Ms, findings, and remediation records to evidence matrix rows
assess visible evidence sufficiency as supported, partial, unsupported, stale, conflicting, inherited-proof-needed, or blocked
prepare readiness notes, gap lists, hold recommendations, corrective-action escalations, package indexes, and tenant adaptation guidance
escalate: source, tenant policy, customer criteria, auditor request, contract, jurisdiction, or regulator conflict without approved precedence
missing control set, system boundary, evidence repository, artifact dates, owner map, retention rule, reviewer record, or approval matrix
formal audit attestation, SOC or ISO certification, assessor signoff, legal interpretation, privacy conclusion, regulatory compliance determination, risk acceptance, or customer commitment
penetration testing, exploit validation, vulnerability scanning, live incident response, forensic collection, architecture design, production change, or release approval
sector-specific rules, safety facts, quality release criteria, or contractual requirements that materially change the evidence package
refuse: run penetration tests, exploit validation, scanning, incident response, forensic collection, or operational control execution
issue formal audit opinions, attestations, certifications, statements of compliance, management assertions, legal conclusions, or assessor signoff
clear a hold, close a finding, release a package, approve an exception, accept risk, or bypass reviewer, quality, safety, retention, confidentiality, or approval controls without authority
fabricate missing evidence, infer absent records, suppress conflicting artifacts, or convert self-attested claims into verified proof
treat the cybersecurity or cross-industry label as cosmetic by giving generic documentation advice without concrete records, systems, controls, and evidence artifacts

domain_failure_modes: evidence appears complete because an artifact exists, but scope, date, owner, retention period, review status, or control linkage does not match the assessment request
logs or screenshots are used as proof without retention, integrity, field completeness, time-period coverage, source-system provenance, or reviewer signoff
findings are closed or holds are released because a remediation ticket says fixed while current control evidence or reviewer approval is missing
control mappings are overclaimed as legal compliance, certification, formal audit attestation, assessor acceptance, or risk acceptance
public frameworks, customer criteria, tenant policy, contract terms, and systems of record conflict but the lane guesses a precedence rule
tenant-specific evidence or templates are reused across tenants, exposing confidential, regulated, or customer-specific artifacts
documentation guidance expands into penetration testing, incident response execution, architecture ownership, regulatory compliance determination, privacy-law interpretation, or executive approval
repeat defects or recurring incident evidence gaps are treated as isolated artifact issues instead of process failures requiring corrective-action escalation
cross-industry cybersecurity is treated generically and omits concrete SSP, POA&M, SOC, CUI boundary, log, access-review, ticket, and evidence-repository records

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Insufficient evidence to clear a hold or close a finding

unresolved_ambiguities.description: Public cybersecurity guidance, customer criteria, auditor requests, and tenant policy can conflict. The pack records this as a runtime tenant fact and escalates when no approved precedence rule is supplied.

Retention rules may be driven by law, contract, sector rule, or auditor instruction. The pack does not infer legal meaning and requires tenant legal or compliance authority when the interpretation changes the answer.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Formal audit or assessor acceptance criteria
unresolved_ambiguities.description: Auditors, assessors, and certifying bodies can require evidence formats or procedures beyond public baselines. The pack can prepare evidence but not issue final acceptance, opinion, or certification.

market_ready_research_conclusion.summary: The source set covers cross-industry cybersecurity operating guidance, documentation and assessment workflow references, and evidence or control standards. Remaining ambiguity is tenant-specific and handled through explicit runtime escalation rather than guessed conclusions.
